• The ban aims to protect domestic poultry farms from cheap Russian imports and support local industry growth.
• Kazakhstan's egg production reached 4.5 billion in 2025, nearing full domestic demand coverage.
• The poultry industry modernization was supported by state aid programs, which the government seeks to protect.
• Import restrictions encourage wholesalers to sign long-term contracts with local suppliers, reducing import dependency.

articles.title: Leveraging precision nutrition with Xtract 6930 - Poultry World
articles.summary:
ADM has developed Xtract 6930, a microencapsulated blend of standardized phytogenic compounds designed to enhance nutrient utilization in broiler poultry. The additive supports precision nutrition in separate-sex rearing by improving feed efficiency, carcass quality, and muscle deposition, particularly tenderloin yield. This innovation addresses variability in nutrient absorption and supports flock uniformity and resilience, which are critical for profitability in modern poultry production. The research signals a strategic move towards integrating phytogenic additives to optimize feed formulations and production outcomes.

• Xtract 6930 improves nutrient utilization and carcass quality in broilers across different diet densities and sexes.
• The additive significantly increased tenderloin yield by 3.3%, enhancing carcass value.
• Separate-sex rearing combined with phytogenic supplementation reduces variability and improves feed efficiency.
• The study involved 1,152 Ross broilers with controlled diet and sex conditions to validate performance benefits.

articles.title: Middle East conflict raises UK poultry cost fears as industry warns of higher food prices
articles.summary:
The British poultry industry warns that the ongoing conflict in the Middle East is causing disruptions in global supply chains, particularly affecting fertiliser and feed ingredient availability and prices. These disruptions are driving up production costs, especially feed and energy expenses, which are critical inputs for poultry farming. The industry calls for accelerated government intervention to mitigate the risk of higher food prices and potential strain on domestic food production. This signals increasing vulnerability of food supply chains to geopolitical conflicts and highlights the need for operational resilience in agriculture.

• Middle East conflict disrupts supply chains for fertiliser, feed additives, and energy.
• Rising fertiliser and feed costs will increase poultry production expenses significantly.
• Energy cost increases, especially for heating poultry sheds, add further financial pressure.
• Potential shortages in poultry medication raise bird welfare and production concerns.
• Industry urges government to expedite poultry sector growth plans to ensure food affordability.

articles.title: Ukraine’s top egg producers post 88% revenue jump on export boom
articles.summary:
Ukraine's top egg producers experienced an 88% revenue increase in 2025, driven primarily by a boom in exports. Despite declining domestic consumption due to population shrinkage, strong demand from European markets—affected by avian influenza and Newcastle disease outbreaks—boosted sales and prices. This growth signals a recovery trajectory for Ukraine's egg industry following wartime disruptions and highlights the strategic importance of export markets in sustaining agricultural profitability.

• Four leading Ukrainian egg manufacturers earned UAH 13.8 billion (€273 million) in 2025, an 88% increase from the previous year.
• Exports accounted for around 40% of production, with 70% of exported eggs going to the European Union.
• EU egg production declines due to disease outbreaks increased demand and prices for Ukrainian eggs.
• Positive price dynamics in Europe contributed significantly to revenue growth for Ukrainian producers.
• The industry is recovering from production capacity losses caused by the war with Russia.

articles.title: Chase or Fade the Rally?: Bobby Molavi on the Equity Bounce
articles.summary:
Goldman Sachs discusses the recent strong rebound in US equities from late-March lows to all-time highs, exploring whether further gains are likely or if traders should consider taking profits. The conversation also examines how upcoming earnings reports might provide insights into the broader economic conditions. This analysis signals Goldman Sachs' strategic focus on market intelligence and investor guidance amid volatile market conditions.

• US equities have rebounded powerfully from late-March lows to reach all-time highs.
• Discussion centers on the potential for continued gains versus profit-taking strategies.
• Earnings reports are highlighted as key indicators of economic health.
• Goldman Sachs positions itself as a thought leader in market analysis and investor advisory.
• The content is informational and does not constitute investment advice.

articles.title: EvoBlow Laser uses lasers to heat preforms
articles.summary:
Sidel launched the EvoBlow Laser machine that uses laser technology instead of conventional halogen lamps to heat PET and recycled PET preforms for container production. This innovation enables more precise heating with up to 36 heating lines, improving packaging lightweighting, strength, and quality while simplifying maintenance. Strategically, it signals a shift towards more sustainable and efficient manufacturing processes in the packaging industry, supporting circular economy goals and operational cost reduction.

• EvoBlow Laser replaces halogen lamps with lasers for preform heating, offering up to 36 heating lines versus 8 in conventional machines.
• The technology enables production of lighter, stronger PET and recycled PET containers with precise targeted heating.
• Simplified machine maintenance due to fewer mechanical parts and no need for ventilation fans or filters.
• Potential to reduce preform waste by 50% and part weight by 10%, supporting sustainability goals.
• Allows use of up to 100% recycled PET in container production.

articles.title: Tumbler finishes 3D printed parts
articles.summary:
Mass Finishing Inc. introduced the HZ-6, a compact centrifugal barrel tumbler designed to simultaneously remove surface roughness and scale from 3D printed parts, including plastics and metals like titanium and Inconel. This machine uses centrifugal force to polish multiple parts quickly, reducing labor costs, production time, and floor space requirements. The innovation enables manufacturers to achieve mirror-like finishes on parts that are difficult to finish by hand, signaling a move toward more automated and efficient post-processing in additive manufacturing workflows.

• HZ-6 tumbler removes surface roughness and scale simultaneously on 3D printed parts.
• Machine uses centrifugal force for fast, simultaneous finishing of multiple parts.
• Reduces labor costs, production times, and floor space requirements.
• Suitable for a variety of materials including titanium, aluminum, Inconel, and carbon steel.
• Enables mirror-like finishes typically unachievable with hand finishing.

articles.title: Regal’s Copilot helps CX teams build AI agents faster
articles.summary:
Regal launched Copilot, a chat-based AI tool designed to drastically reduce the time and complexity involved in building, testing, and deploying AI agents for customer experience teams. By enabling natural language input and leveraging knowledge from millions of calls, Copilot automates agent creation, cutting development time from weeks to potentially days. This innovation signals a shift towards more accessible, agentic AI development tools that empower non-technical users and accelerate enterprise AI adoption in regulated industries. It also highlights the growing importance of AI orchestration platforms that integrate operational knowledge and best practices.

• Regal Copilot simplifies AI agent creation via a chat interface, reducing build time from weeks to much shorter periods.
• Copilot uses natural language input and can ingest call recordings or scripts to generate AI agent drafts.
• The tool leverages Regal’s Model Context Protocol to access insights from 400 million calls across regulated industries.
• Copilot supports agentic AI agents capable of end-to-end customer interactions, not just FAQ responses.
• This innovation addresses scalability challenges for enterprises with hundreds of AI use cases.

articles.title: Computer-Using Agents: Visualizing access to legacy systems
articles.summary:
Computer-Using Agents (CUAs) represent a new AI capability that enables AI models to interact with software interfaces visually, mimicking human actions like clicking and typing. This innovation addresses the challenge of automating tasks on legacy systems and across multiple applications without requiring deep API integrations. CUAs have immediate relevance for unified communications and contact centers by automating error-prone, manual cross-application workflows, improving efficiency and reducing costs. While promising, CUAs are still emerging technology and should be approached with realistic expectations compared to traditional RPA.

• CUAs enable AI to operate on the visual layer of enterprise software, bypassing API limitations.
• They facilitate automation of complex, multi-step tasks across legacy and modern systems alike.
• CUAs are particularly valuable in unified communications and contact center environments to reduce manual 'swivel-chair' work.
• Major AI providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Amazon are developing CUA capabilities.
• CUAs are more adaptive than traditional RPA but remain less robust than clean API integrations.

articles.title: Universal Music Group launches Everything Jazz, a new global digital platform for jazz music and culture - Music Business Worldwide
articles.summary:
Universal Music Group has launched Everything Jazz, a global digital platform and online store dedicated to jazz music and culture, developed in partnership with iconic jazz labels. This platform centralizes curated retail offerings and editorial content, targeting a global audience with access to both catalog and new releases, including premium vinyl series. The initiative signals UMG's strategic expansion in the jazz music market by leveraging digital channels to engage fans worldwide and deepen cultural storytelling. The platform's phased rollout across multiple countries and its educational content initiative underscore a commitment to growing jazz's global presence and fanbase.

• Launch of Everything Jazz, a dedicated global digital platform for jazz music and culture by Universal Music Group.
• Partnership with renowned jazz labels such as Blue Note, Verve, and ECM to offer curated retail and editorial content.
• Phased international rollout includes local stores in Europe, Japan, Australia, Canada, and the US.
• Platform features premium vinyl reissues and exclusive artist interviews, enhancing fan engagement and storytelling.
• First major initiative (Re)Discover Jazz offers digital lessons to educate and expand the jazz audience during Jazz Appreciation Month.

articles.title: Cambodia inducts enlarged Type?056 corvette from China
articles.summary:
Cambodia has recently inducted an enlarged variant of the Chinese-built Type 056A corvette at Ream Naval Base, marking a significant enhancement in its naval capabilities. This vessel is approximately 5 meters longer than standard Type 056A corvettes, positioning it among the largest of its class outside China. The induction signals Cambodia's strategic move to expand and modernize its maritime defense assets, enabling extended patrols and improved naval operations. This development also reflects China's growing role as a defense supplier in Southeast Asia.

• Cambodia received an enlarged Type 056A corvette from China, about 95 meters in length.
• The vessel is larger than standard Type 056 and Type 056A corvettes operated by China and other export customers.
• The ship was likely commissioned into Cambodian service in early April 2026 at Ream Naval Base.
• This induction enhances Cambodia's maritime patrol and naval operational capabilities.
• The move underscores China's expanding defense export footprint in Southeast Asia.

articles.title: Hargreaves Lansdown’s Derren Nathan: A stellar year for the IPO watchlist
articles.summary:
Hargreaves Lansdown’s Derren Nathan reflects on a strong year for the IPO watchlist, highlighting significant market interest in sectors such as space, artificial intelligence, and banking. The mention of the Artemis II mission underscores the renewed enthusiasm for space-related investments, signaling a broader investor appetite for innovative and high-growth sectors. This commentary signals strategic market positioning around emerging technologies and sectors driving IPO activity. It also reflects investor sentiment and market dynamics influencing capital markets and public offerings.

• Notable focus on space, AI, and banking sectors as key drivers

articles.title: MMD strikes deal with CiDI to bring autonomy to TraxIQ platform - International Mining
articles.summary:
MMD Group Limited has entered a Memorandum of Agreement with CiDi Inc. to integrate advanced autonomous driving technology into the TraxIQ platform, aiming to revolutionize material handling in mining operations. This partnership combines MMD's expertise in materials handling with CiDi's autonomy hardware and software to enable retrofit kits for autonomous mining equipment. Strategically, this signals a shift towards system-level integration in mining logistics, enhancing operational efficiency, scalability, and energy management. The collaboration also underscores a commitment to global commercialization and validation of autonomous material handling in mining environments.

• MMD and CiDi signed an agreement to integrate autonomous driving tech into TraxIQ.
• CiDi provides sensing and compute systems for autonomous control within TraxIQ.
• The partnership enables retrofit kits to convert existing mining equipment to autonomous operation.
• Focus on validating and deploying the system globally, building on experience in China.
• The initiative aims to improve efficiency, reduce energy use, and support scalable mining operations.

articles.title: How Chelsea star Cole Palmer is shaking up sponsorships
articles.summary:
Chelsea and England footballer Cole Palmer has trademarked his signature goal celebration 'Cold Palmer' and launched his own logo, signaling a shift in how young athletes build and leverage personal brands off the pitch. This move exemplifies a growing trend where athletes actively control and monetize their personal brand identities, influencing sponsorship and brand interaction strategies. It highlights the increasing importance of athlete-driven brand assets in marketing and sponsorship deals, suggesting future athletes will follow suit to enhance their commercial value.

• Cole Palmer trademarked his signature 'Cold Palmer' goal celebration in late 2025.
• He launched his own personal logo earlier in 2026.
• This represents a new approach for athletes to build and protect personal brand assets.
• Brand advisors predict this trend will become more common among athletes.
• It changes how brands engage with athletes, focusing more on personal brand collaborations.

articles.title: Marketing Week to co-host sessions with Campaign at Cannes Lions
articles.summary:
Marketing Week will co-host sessions with Campaign at Cannes Lions 2026 as part of Haymarket's portfolio activations during the Festival of Creativity. This collaboration marks the first joint effort since Haymarket's acquisition of Marketing Week and other marketing brands from Centaur Media. The initiative enhances Haymarket's presence and influence at a major global advertising event, leveraging multiple editorial brands and partners. It signals a strategic move to consolidate and amplify Haymarket's marketing communications ecosystem through event-based collaboration and content creation.

• Marketing Week and Campaign will co-host sessions at Cannes Lions 2026.
• The collaboration is part of Haymarket's activations for its marketing and communications brands.
• This is the first joint effort since Haymarket acquired Marketing Week and related brands.
• Campaign House will serve as the venue, hosting multiple editorial teams and partners.
• The initiative strengthens Haymarket's strategic positioning in the global advertising festival.

articles.title: Netflix in talks to 'expand the relationship' with NFL – co-CEO
articles.summary:
Netflix is exploring expanding its relationship with the NFL beyond its current Christmas Day game package, potentially acquiring rights to additional games such as the Thanksgiving eve game or the NFL season opener. This move signals Netflix's intent to strengthen its sports content offering as a tool for subscriber acquisition and retention. The discussions occur amid broader NFL TV rights scrutiny by the US Department of Justice, highlighting the competitive and regulatory environment around sports broadcasting rights. Additionally, Netflix's leadership changes and strong financial performance provide context for its strategic positioning in content expansion.

• Netflix is in talks to expand NFL game access beyond the Christmas Day package.
• Potential games of interest include the Thanksgiving eve game and the NFL season opener.
• NFL's TV rights deals are under antitrust investigation by the US Department of Justice.
• Netflix founder Reed Hastings will not seek board re-election, focusing on philanthropy.
• Netflix posted strong Q1 2026 revenues and maintains robust full-year guidance.

articles.title: Singapore's Circles seeks Nasdaq listing, focuses on US expansion
articles.summary:
Singapore-based digital telecom platform provider Circles is planning a Nasdaq listing within 18 to 24 months, contingent on securing significant wins in the US market, which represents about 40% of its addressable market. The company is expanding its US presence through customer growth and an imminent acquisition, aiming to leverage its AI-powered platform to address telecom operators' 5G monetization challenges. Circles is also strengthening its leadership and governance structures in preparation for public listing, positioning itself to capitalize on the global shift of telcos toward digital transformation and 5G service innovation. This move signals Circles' strategic intent to scale globally by deepening its foothold in the critical US telecom market.

• Circles targets Nasdaq listing within 18-24 months, dependent on US market success.
• US market accounts for approximately 40% of Circles' total addressable market.
• Company is close to a US acquisition to boost market presence.
• Circles leverages an AI-powered platform to help telcos monetize 5G and transition to techco models.
• Leadership and governance enhancements are underway to support public market readiness.

articles.title:
Siemens to demonstrate
in Hannover how industrial AI is revolution ...
articles.summary:
Siemens is showcasing how industrial AI and digital twin technologies are transforming manufacturing at Hannover Messe 2026. The initiative highlights AI-driven automation from design to production, enhancing productivity, innovation speed, and resource efficiency amid challenges like skills shortages and market volatility. Siemens demonstrates applications in robotics, supply chain resilience, and flexible manufacturing, including collaborations with consumer goods companies like PepsiCo and Pringles. This signals a strategic push to embed AI deeply into industrial operations, driving competitive advantage and future-proofing European manufacturing.

• Siemens is building an industrial AI operating system integrating AI across design, engineering, production, and operations.
• Showcases include AI-powered robotics, supply chain transparency, and a modular pop-up factory concept for rapid market response.
• Consumer goods industry use cases demonstrate AI-enabled digitalization of value chains for faster, reliable product launches.
• Future manufacturing vision includes autonomous production with AI chat interfaces, AI-coordinated design, and humanoid robot logistics.
• Focus on addressing energy efficiency challenges in data centers as part of the broader industrial AI ecosystem.

articles.title: Disney+ Premium, Hulu, ESPN+ promotional subscription FAQs
articles.summary:
Verizon is offering a promotional subscription bundle that includes Disney+ Premium, Hulu, and ESPN+ at no extra charge for customers on its 5G Get More or 5G Play More mobile plans. This bundle, valued at $24.99 per month, aims to enhance Verizon's value proposition by integrating popular streaming services with its mobile offerings. Additionally, customers without these plans can access the Disney bundle with ads for $10 per month through Verizon's myPlan. This initiative signals Verizon's strategic move to leverage content partnerships to drive customer acquisition and retention in a competitive 5G market.

• Verizon includes Disney+ Premium, Hulu, and ESPN+ for free with select 5G mobile plans.
• The bundle is valued at $24.99 per month, enhancing the attractiveness of Verizon's 5G plans.
• Customers without qualifying plans can subscribe to the Disney bundle with ads for $10 per month.
• Only one promotional subscription is allowed per eligible Verizon mobile account.
• The offer strengthens Verizon's competitive positioning by bundling popular streaming content with mobile services.

articles.title: Walt Disney Studios Wows CinemaCon® Attendees at 2026 Gathering in Las Vegas | The Walt Disney Company
articles.summary:
The Walt Disney Studios presented a high-profile showcase at CinemaCon 2026 in Las Vegas, highlighting upcoming theatrical releases from its seven major studios. The event emphasized Disney's dominant box office performance in 2025, with three of the biggest Hollywood movies and a global box office lead for nine of the past ten years. This signals Disney's strategic focus on theatrical experiences and storytelling excellence to sustain and grow its market leadership. The studio also highlighted its extensive portfolio of billion-dollar grossing titles and longer theatrical runs compared to competitors, reinforcing its strong distribution and audience engagement capabilities.

• Disney Studios showcased upcoming theatrical releases from seven major studios at CinemaCon 2026.
• Disney led the global box office in 2025 with three top-grossing films, including Zootopia 2 and Avatar: Fire and Ash.
• The studio has been number one at the global box office for nine of the past ten years.
• Disney's films have longer theatrical runs, averaging 57 days, enhancing box office revenue potential.
• The company emphasized high-quality storytelling as a key differentiator driving audience engagement worldwide.

articles.title: Physical AI Is Coming to the Factory Floor, Connectivity May Decide How Fast It Arrives
articles.summary:
Manufacturers are progressing from traditional AI applications like predictive maintenance and quality inspection to an emerging phase called physical AI, which enables systems to perform real-world tasks and learn from environmental interactions. This shift represents a strategic move toward AI systems that continuously improve through experience rather than static data analysis. The speed of adoption may depend heavily on connectivity infrastructure, highlighting the importance of robust network capabilities on the factory floor. This development signals a transformative evolution in manufacturing operations, emphasizing autonomous physical interaction and adaptive learning.

• Physical AI represents a new phase of AI focused on real-world task execution and learning through interaction.
• Manufacturers have historically invested in AI for operational improvements like predictive maintenance and quality control.
• Physical AI systems rely on sensors and environmental feedback to continuously improve performance.
• Connectivity quality may be a critical factor influencing the speed of physical AI adoption in factories.

articles.title: New Technology Ensures Fast, Accurate Harness Assembly
articles.summary:
The announcement highlights new products designed to improve wiring harness assembly, including a pneumatic banding tool and a motorized pull-force tester. These tools address key manufacturing challenges such as supply chain disruptions, rising material costs, and workforce shortages by enhancing process efficiency and accuracy. Strategically, this signals a focus on operational innovation through automation and improved tooling to optimize internal manufacturing workflows. The deployment of these tools can help wiring harness shops maintain quality and throughput in a challenging production environment.

• Introduction of new pneumatic banding tool and motorized pull-force tester for harness assembly
• Tools aim to improve speed and accuracy in wire processing and assembly
• Addresses manufacturing challenges like supply chain issues and labor shortages
• Supports operational efficiency and workflow optimization in wiring harness production

articles.title: Accenture Invests in General Robotics to Scale Physical AI and Humanoid Robotics Deployment
articles.summary:
Accenture has made a strategic investment in General Robotics through its venture arm to advance physical AI and humanoid robotics deployment in manufacturing and logistics. This partnership aims to develop enterprise-grade robotics intelligence to help organizations better integrate and manage robotic systems across factory operations. The move signals Accenture's commitment to scaling autonomous systems and enhancing operational efficiency in industrial environments. It reflects broader industry trends toward automation and AI-driven robotics in supply chain and manufacturing sectors.

• Accenture invested in General Robotics to expand physical AI and humanoid robotics capabilities.
• The partnership focuses on developing enterprise-grade robotics intelligence for factories.
• Target industries include manufacturing and logistics operators aiming to scale autonomous systems.
• The collaboration supports more efficient integration and management of robotic systems in operations.

articles.title: Danone faces ‘forest policy’ backlash as Harrogate expansion threatens 1,000-tree community woodland
articles.summary:
Danone's plan to expand its Harrogate Spring Water bottling plant involves removing approximately 1,000 trees from a 20-year-old community woodland, sparking significant backlash. This move contradicts Danone's publicly stated Forest Policy commitments to deforestation-free supply chains and forest-positive status, raising questions about the company's environmental credibility. The controversy highlights tensions between industrial growth and environmental stewardship, with local and national figures urging Danone to reconsider to preserve biodiversity and community trust. The final planning decision by local councillors will determine the future of the woodland and Danone's environmental reputation.

• Danone plans to cut down around 1,000 trees from a community woodland to expand its bottling plant.
• The expansion contradicts Danone's Forest Policy pledges of deforestation-free supply chains by 2025 and forest-positive status by 2030.
• Local community, environmentalists, and celebrities publicly criticize the plan, emphasizing ecological and social value of the woodland.
• The decision rests with local councillors, with significant reputational risks for Danone depending on the outcome.

articles.title: White House Pushes ‘Action-Oriented’ Cyber Strategy to Deter Threats
articles.summary:
The White House, through the Office of the National Cyber Director, has introduced an action-oriented National Cyber Strategy alongside an executive order aimed at deterring cyber adversaries by imposing strict consequences on cybercriminals. This initiative emphasizes coordinated federal efforts using diplomatic, economic, law enforcement, and technical tools to protect American citizens from cyber-enabled fraud and cybercrime. The strategy also includes establishing a Victims Restoration Program to compensate victims from seized criminal assets, signaling a strong stance on cybersecurity enforcement and victim support. Strategically, this reflects a shift towards proactive deterrence and comprehensive government response to transnational cyber threats.

• The National Cyber Strategy focuses on deterring adversaries by increasing the risks and consequences of cyberattacks on American interests.
• An executive order titled Combating Cybercrime, Fraud, and Predatory Schemes Against American Citizens was signed to operationalize the strategy.
• The federal government will leverage diplomatic, economic, law enforcement, and technical tools to respond aggressively to cybercrime organizations and complicit foreign entities.
• A Victims Restoration Program will be established to repay victims of cyber-enabled fraud using funds seized from transnational criminal organizations.
• Improved information sharing and regulatory reform are identified as critical to preventing and defending against cybercrime.

articles.title: How Anthurium got its color, wax, and floral form
articles.summary:
Researchers assembled chromosome-level genomes of two Anthurium species and used multi-omics analyses to uncover how genome evolution, pigment metabolism, and wax biosynthesis collectively determine key ornamental traits such as spathe color, spadix form, and floral longevity. They identified genetic regulators and metabolic pathways involved in anthocyanin pigment accumulation and wax deposition, linking these to flower development stages and vase life. This integrated framework advances understanding of the complex genetic and biochemical networks shaping Anthurium's commercial traits, enabling future breeding and biotechnological improvements.

• Chromosome-level genomes of Anthurium andraeanum and A. scherzerianum revealed large genomes with chromosomal rearrangements and two whole-genome duplications.
• Genetic clustering of 179 accessions showed two major groups not fully aligned with traditional horticultural classifications.
• Dynamic transcription factor networks involving SOC1 and AGL6 genes regulate spadix development and floral diversification.
• Anthocyanin biosynthesis pathways with 33 enzyme genes coordinate pigment accumulation and color variation through metabolite ratios.
• Wax biosynthesis genes CER3, KCS1, and KCS3 correlate with wax deposition levels and longer floral vase life.

articles.title: Teaching neural networks where errors matter
articles.summary:
Researchers developed volume weighting physics-informed neural networks (VW-PINNs) to improve training of neural networks solving partial differential equations (PDEs) by weighting residual errors based on the physical volume represented by collocation points. This approach prevents overfitting in densely sampled regions and ensures balanced error reduction across the computational domain. VW-PINNs successfully solved challenging fluid dynamics problems, such as flow over a circular cylinder and airfoil, where conventional PINNs failed. The method enhances robustness under nonuniform sampling and adaptive meshfree settings, promising improved reliability for physics-informed AI in engineering applications.

• VW-PINNs weight PDE residuals by the volume occupied by collocation points to address loss imbalance in training.
• The method prevents over-learning in densely sampled regions, ensuring uniform error reduction across the domain.
• VW-PINNs succeeded in solving fluid flow problems (circular cylinder, NACA0012 airfoil) where standard PINNs failed.
• The approach includes a kernel density estimation algorithm for volume estimation in meshfree contexts.
• VW-PINNs improve accuracy and computational speed in forward and inverse PDE problems, enhancing physics-informed AI applicability.

articles.title: Verizon Fios | Fiber-Optic Internet | Plans Start at $35/mo | Verizon

articles.title: Verizon Newsroom
articles.summary:
Verizon Communications reported strong financial results and customer growth in Q2 2025, leading to an upward revision of its financial guidance for adjusted EBITDA, adjusted EPS, and free cash flow. The announcement highlights Verizon's robust operational performance and market position as the largest mobility and broadband provider in the U.S. Additionally, Verizon is enhancing its customer offerings with new AI-powered smartphone availability and innovative marketing tied to the FIFA World Cup 2026.
